articles.title: a look inside mission control
articles.description: As four astronauts whiz toward a flyby of the Moon, looking out for them are mission control experts using cutting-edge technology and lessons learned from the Apollo program 50 years ago.
articles.content:
As four astronauts whiz toward a flyby of the Moon, looking out for them are mission control experts using cutting-edge technology and lessons learned from the Apollo program 50 years ago.
At the Johnson Space Centre in Houston, a team of engineers a... [3911 chars]

articles.title: New tech to fortify British era storm water drains, prevent floods
articles.description: Experts say the technology will minimise excavation without disrupting traffic, phase 1 to be completed in 2027 | Mumbai news
articles.content: The British-era drains will be fortified using geopolymer lining trenchless technology. Geo-polymer is a mineral-based chemical material made from aluminosilicate powders (such as fly ash or metakaolin) mixed with alkaline solution, which reacts chem... [2300 chars]

articles.title: Farmer and First Nations artist collaborate to create beautiful, cultural fashion
articles.description: When a farmer and a First Nations artist join forces, something beautiful emerges — thanks to Japanese knitting technology.
articles.content:
In far-western Victoria, down a winding track amid rolling hills and glorious gum trees, something surprisingly beautiful and unexpected is happening.
So beautiful, that the creations emerging from an isolated farmhouse in the town of Pigeon Ponds re... [3664 chars]

articles.title: ‘Millet’ recipes developed with Indian technology served by international food chains: Dr Jitendra
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent MYSURU, Apr 3 : India’s millet push received a dual institutional boost today as the Centre moved to scale both technology and grassroots capacity, with Union Minister for Science and Technology Dr. Jitendra Singh highlighting Millet recipes developed with Indian technology are being served by international food chains, including McDonalds. The Centre is now working on developing similar sustainable food recipes from “Kalari” , a popular cheese product originating from Udhampur in J&K, the Minister informed. During a […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Correspondent
MYSURU, Apr 3 : India’s millet push received a dual institutional boost today as the Centre moved to scale both technology and grassroots capacity, with Union Minister for Science and Technology Dr. Jitendra Singh highlighting... [3797 chars]

articles.title: IUST organises cybersecurity awareness program
articles.description: Excelsior Correspondent AWANTIPORA, Apr 3: The Office of the Dean Outreach, Islamic University of Science and Technology (IUST), organised a cybersecurity awareness program for students and apprentices of the University. The program was sponsored under the Information Security Education and Awareness (ISEA) Project of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY). The program was conducted by experts from the CDAC-Mohali as part of a national initiative to promote cybersecurity awareness and responsible digital practices. More than a hundred participants, […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Correspondent
AWANTIPORA, Apr 3: The Office of the Dean Outreach, Islamic University of Science and Technology (IUST), organised a cybersecurity awareness program for students and apprentices of the University.
The program was sponsored und... [1495 chars]

articles.title: OpenAI Isn’t Just Buying A Podcast It’s Buying Influence In The AI Narrative War
articles.description: INSIDE THIS REPORT A century-old strategy is quietly resurfacing as tech giants move to control not just platforms—but the conversations surrounding them. OpenAI’s acquisition of a niche but influential AI talk show signals a deeper shift in how power is exercised in the digital age. The question is no longer who builds the technology—but who OpenAI’s acquisition of TBPN signals a strategic move to shape AI narratives, reflecting a broader shift in how influence and technology intersect.
articles.content:
INSIDE THIS REPORT
A century-old strategy is quietly resurfacing as tech giants move to control not just platforms—but the conversations surrounding them.
OpenAI’s acquisition of a niche but influential AI talk show signals a deeper shift in how powe... [5579 chars]

articles.title: JDGD Saraf Trust felicitates winners of Conscious Voice Essay Award at MIET
articles.description: Excelsior Sports Correspondent JAMMU, Apr 3: The JDGD Saraf Trust felicitated winners of The Conscious Voice Essay Award 2025 at a function hosted by Model Institute of Engineering & Technology. Rohan Nagra secured the first prize and Himanshu Chib the second, both from MIET, receiving cash awards of Rs 15,000 and Rs 10,000 respectively from Trustee Rachna Vinod. Certificates of Excellence were awarded to Tanisha Kohli of Indian Institute of Mass Communication, along with MIET students Aniket Gupta, Ishween Kour […]
articles.content:
Excelsior Sports Correspondent
JAMMU, Apr 3: The JDGD Saraf Trust felicitated winners of The Conscious Voice Essay Award 2025 at a function hosted by Model Institute of Engineering & Technology.
Rohan Nagra secured the first prize and Himanshu Chib t... [878 chars]

articles.title: MUSC Children’s Hospital debuts imaging machine for spinal conditions
articles.description: The Medical University of South Carolina Children’s Health Hospital has new technology to make doctor visits easier for patients with spinal conditions.
articles.content:
NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCSC) — The Medical University of South Carolina Children’s Health Hospital has new technology to make doctor visits easier for patients with spinal conditions.
The EOS imaging machine is geared toward children with conditions... [1118 chars]

articles.title: H2 Water Technologies Ltd. Announces Issuance of Key U.S. Patent for Producing Hydrogen Rich Water; Identifies Echo Water's "Hydrogen Prebiotic Stick” as Infringing Product
articles.description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia, April 03, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- H2 Water Technologies Ltd., a leading innovator in hydrogen water research and technology, today announced that the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) has issued U.S. Patent No. 12,588,693, titled ”Composition for Producing Hydrogen Rich Water.' This newly issued patent adds to H2 Water Technologies' broad intellectual property portfolio and protects an important technology for health-minded consumers.
articles.content: VANCOUVER, British Columbia, April 03, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- H2 Water Technologies Ltd., a leading innovator in hydrogen water research and technology, today announced that the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) has issued U.S. Pate... [2118 chars]

articles.title: How AI Will Help People Instead of Taking Their Jobs Away
articles.description: Aryan Singh Vashista anjukumariartandcraft@gmail.com In the recent past, there has been much talk about the concept of artificial intelligence and how it will take over human jobs. However, looking back on the history of how technology has evolved over the years, we can get a better understanding of how artificial intelligence will not only not take our jobs, but also help us. The main idea behind the concept of artificial intelligence is to help people, not replace them. In most […]
articles.content:
Aryan Singh Vashista
anjukumariartandcraft@gmail.com

and how it will take over human jobs. However, looking back on the history of how
